oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Collaborating with outside expertise to bring value to your practice model
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Groves, John
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: outside expertise problem-solving
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: In addition to traditional services, many veterinary profes­sionals play key roles in the management and decision-making processes of complex adaptive and evolving production sys­tems. Seeking diverse perspectives by accessing expertise that traditionally exists outside conventional practice models can be beneficial when addressing long-standing, complex and chal­lenging problems. Expanding professional networks to access specialized knowledge can broaden understanding, and result in different problem-solving approaches that spark innovation and creativity. The novel perspective and unbiased insights gained by utilizing outside expertise can enhance the problem-solving skill set of the practice and increase the value and rel­evance to clientele. This paper will describe how I have utilized outside expertise professionally to bring problem-solving ca­pacity to my practice.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 41-42
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
